economic_notes: Band owns Xeni Gwet'in Enterprises (economic development/excavating). Investments in Cariboo Indian Enterprises Ltd., Tsilhqot'in Economic Development Corp., Tsilhqot'in Forest Products Inc., Tsilhqot'in Economic Advancement GP Ltd., and Dandzen Development Limited Partnership (all at cost). Supreme Court of Canada ruled favourably on aboriginal rights and title case. Band has $36,389,000 in accumulated legal fees related to asserting aboriginal title, contingent on outside funding.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2021 (not 2024). Auditor name not included in the provided text. 1224366 B.C. Ltd. is fully consolidated as a subsidiary. Capital lease obligation of $438,980 (RBC) is separate from long-term debt. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,676,444 + CMHC $57,732 + Social housing $86,400 = $3,820,576; OSR = $12,240,389 - $3,820,576 = $8,419,813. Annual debt service includes long-term debt repayments $74,241 and capital lease repayments $124,108 = $198,349. No schedule of remuneration provided. Deferred revenue includes $2,977,481 from CMHC and $207,385 from ISC. Large contingent liability of $36,389,000 in legal fees related to aboriginal title litigation.
